news.title: See full list of 2025 Album Of The Year nominees.
Check out the artists making their debut in this 2025 ceremony.
news.content:
The 29th Millennium Awards is shaping up to be a historic night for newcomers. While legendary figures often dominate the headlines, the 2025 nominations list is overflowing with fresh talent making their mark across every possible genre.

• The Heavy Hitters
In the major categories, the competition is fiercer than ever. Deftones achieve a career milestone with their first-ever 'Album Of The Year' nomination, while also dominating the Rock/Metal field. In the 'Record Of The Year' race, HUNTR/X makes a stunning entrance, also securing a nod in Visual Media. Rosé proves her power as a solo artist; following her global success, she is a first-time nominee in 'Record Of The Year' and 'Best Collaboration' for the hit 'APT.' with Bruno Mars.

• Best New Artist & Pop Breakthroughs
The 'Best New Artist' (BNA) category is a true battle of titans this year. The list features the multifaceted Leon Thomas, who also earned nods in R&B, Jazz, and as 'Songwriter of The Year.' Joining him are pop sensations Addison Rae (also recognized in Pop Performance), Jade (Dance Pop Recording), Lola Young (who also landed a 'Song of the Year' nomination), Olivia Dean, Tate McRae, and Sombr. Ravyn Lenae also shines as a BNA contender with additional presence in R&B and Melodic Rap.

• Rock, Metal & R&B Revolutions
The Rock and Metal fields are packed with debutants, including Turnstile, Garbage, Deafheaven, Jinjer, Sleep Token, and Spiritbox. Meanwhile, the R&B field welcomes soul-stirring debuts from Durand Bernarr, Jane Handcock, Leon Bridges, and Bilal.

• Global, Latin & African Influence
The Latin field sees a massive influx of talent with Fuerza Regida, Xênia França, Don L, and Negra Li, alongside the collaborative force of João Gomes and Mestrinho. From the African continent, Omah Lay, Davido, Qing Madi, and Wretch 32 are making their first big splash at the ceremony.

• In the Global Music field, newcomers include Aditya Rikhari, Katseye, Kenshi Yonezu, Olly, Juli, Tinariwen, Gims, and Mata. The Jazz field also welcomes a wave of new faces: Boz Scaggs, Cécile McLorin Salvant, June McDoom, Kate Davis, Christian McBride, Samara Joy, Laufey, and Tyreek McDole.

• Specialized Success
Dance music enthusiasts will see Anyma, Argy, Son of Son, and Caribou making their debut in the Dance categories. In the Country field, legends and newcomers collide: the iconic Dolly Parton receives her first-ever 'Country Performance' nomination, joined by first-timers Jelly Roll and Kelsea Ballerini.

Visual Media and special performances also highlight Pomme (collaborating with Stromae), Cynthia Erivo for her work on 'Wicked,' and Caroline Polachek for her vocal performance on 'On the Beach.' Lastly, the Rap field sees fresh energy from Skepta and PlaqueBoyMax, marking a new era for the 29th Millennium Awards.

news.title: Achievements from The 29th Millennium's Nominations: Bad Bunny, Kendrick, Lady Gaga and much more!
news.content:
The 29th edition of the Millennium Awards (2025) has established itself as a landmark of diversity and creative power, revealing a landscape of absolute dominance both on stage and behind the scenes. This year's nominations list reflects the balance between global stardom and the technical mastery that defines the contemporary music industry.

At the top of the pyramid among lead artists, Bad Bunny leads with an impressive 11 nominations. The Puerto Rican star is present in elite categories, including Album of the Year for 'Debí Tirar Más Fotos', Record of the Year, and Artist of the Year. Right behind, Kendrick Lamar reaffirms his cultural relevance with 10 nominations, securing his place in all 'General Field' categories and dominating the Rap field.

However, the true record of this edition lies with the architects of sound. Andrew Watt and Jack Antonoff are tied as the most nominated individuals overall, with 13 nominations each, reflecting their influence on multiple high-profile projects. Other fundamental names in production and engineering, such as Sounwave, MAG, and Cirkut, secured 10 nominations each, proving that technical excellence is the backbone of this 2025 crop.

Among pop stars, Lady Gaga shines with 8 nominations, driven by the success of 'Mayhem' and the visual and sonic impact of 'Abracadabra'. The new generation also secured its space: Leon Thomas made history as 'Best New Artist' and 'Songwriter of the Year', while British artist Lola Young accumulated 6 nominations. To crown the ceremony, the legendary Elton John was honored with the 'Diamond Icon Award', totaling 8 nominations and proving that his legacy remains more alive than ever in the history of the awards.

news.title: 2025 Millennium Awards: Bad Bunny's Historic Sweep and Genre Highlights
news.content:
The 27th Millennium Awards ceremony has concluded, leaving behind a trail of historic moments and a definitive map of the musical landscape today. From a legendary sweep in the General Field to technical breakthroughs, here is the full analysis of the night’s victors.

GENERAL FIELD
The night was defined by Bad Bunny's historic sweep. The Puerto Rican superstar took home Album of the Year (Debí Tirar Más Fotos), Record of the Year (DTMF), Song of the Year (DTMF), and the prestigious Artist of the Year trophy. This landslide victory is more than just a statistical feat; it signals a tectonic shift where Spanish has moved from being a barrier to the lingua franca of global pop, reflecting an academy that now prioritizes cultural impact over linguistic borders. Joining the winner's circle, Leon Thomas was crowned Best New Artist, while the cinematic Luther, by Kendrick Lamar and SZA, won Best Collaboration, highlighting how top-tier partnerships remain the engine of commercial innovation.

POP, DANCE & ELECTRONIC FIELD
In the Pop arena, Lady Gaga triumphed with Best Pop Album for Mayhem and Best Dance Pop Recording for Abracadabra. Gaga’s victory reaffirms her status as the supreme architect of conceptual pop in an era of fleeting viral trends. Lola Young secured Best Pop Performance for Messy, bringing an emotional rawness that serves as a necessary counterpoint to electronic perfection, while the legendary partnership of Elton John and Brandi Carlile won Best Pop Song for Who Believes in Angels?. In the electronic world, Anyma, Argy, and Son of Son took Best Performance (Voices in My Head), Kaytranada won Best Album (Ain't No Damn Way!), and Gesaffelstein was honored for Best Remixed Recording, showing that the genre is splitting between dancefloor euphoria and groove refinement.

LATÍN & GLOBAL FIELD
Bad Bunny continued his dominance here, winning Best Latín Performance, Song, and Album. However, regional diversity shone with Gaby Amarantos winning Best Latín Regional Album for Rock Doido. The Urban categories saw a major win for the Brazilian collective BaianaSystem, Alice Carvalho, and Anitta (Urban Performance) and Rauw Alejandro (Urban Album), a clear validation that Brazil and Puerto Rico are the twin centers of urban reinvention. Globally, Stromae and Pomme took the Performance win for Ma Meillure Ennemie, while Tinariwen secured the Best Global Music Album award, proving that rhythmic authenticity still outweighs pasteurized production.

R&B, RAP & AFROBEATS FIELD
Rap was a battlefield of titans: Kendrick Lamar won Best Rap Song (Reincarnated) and Best Melodic Rap Performance (Luther), while Tyler, The Creator won Best Rap Album (Chromakopia). This division shows an academy that values both Kendrick's narrative depth and Tyler's sonic experimentation. A standout international win went to BK, JXNV$, and Trio Mocoto for Best Rap Performance, elevating Brazilian hip-hop to the world’s elite echelon. In R&B, Giveon took Best Performance, Kehlani won Best Song, and Leon Thomas won Best Album. The Progressive R&B trophies went to Rochelle Jordan (Performance) and Kelela (Album), while Wizkid dominated the Afrobeats field, consolidating Nigeria as an unbeatable cultural export powerhouse.

ALTERNATIVE, ROCK & METAL FIELD
Bon Iver dominated the Alternative field with wins in both Performance and Album (Sable, Fable), confirming that minimalism and introspection still hold massive space in a noisy industry. Fiona Apple won Best Alternative Song with her always-sharp songwriting. Wet Leg proved their staying power by winning Best Rock Performance and Best Rock Album. The Metal field belonged to Deftones, who won Best Rock Song (Infinite Source) and Best Metal Album (Private Music), marking a rare creative renaissance for veterans of the genre, while Spiritbox secured Best Metal Performance, pointing toward the style's progressive future.

COUNTRY & JAZZ FIELDS
Julien Baker and Torres were the stars of the Country field, taking home Best Performance and Best Album with a sound that defies traditional Nashville conventions, while Tyler Childers won Best Country Song. In Jazz, Cécile McLorin Salvant achieved a double win for Best Jazz Album and Best Jazz Performance (alongside June McDoom and Kate Davis), reaffirming that the genre remains the bastion of technical purity and vocal virtuosity.

TECHNICAL & ACHIEVEMENT FIELDS
The architects of sound were also celebrated: MAG was named Producer of the Year and Leon Thomas won Songwriter of the Year, a win that underlines the importance of multifaceted creators in today's streaming ecosystem. The team behind Bon Iver's Sable, Fable took home the Best Engineered Album award, recognizing the sonic precision needed to create immersive atmospheres. In the Achievement field, Elton John received the Diamond Icon Award and Mon Laferte was recognized for Best Vocal Performance, a reminder that raw talent remains at the heart of the Millennium brand.

VISUAL MEDIA FIELD
Closing the night's honors, Tyler, The Creator won Best Music Video for Noid, a work that reaffirms his vision as an authorial director. The Best Music for Visual Media award went to the innovative team behind Golden: HUNTR/X, Ejae, Audrey Nuna, and Rei Ami, proving that the synergy between music and image is now more vital than ever for the pop narrative.

The general analysis of the 2025 winners reveals an awards ceremony that has finally embraced globalization in its entirety. Bad Bunny's dominance is not just a personal record, but a symbol of the unwavering strength of Latin music at the center of the world stage. At the same time, the inclusion of significant wins from artists in Brazil, Nigeria, South Korea, and Europe in technical and genre categories shows that the Millennium Awards has transformed into a real thermometer of global creativity. The balance between recognizing legends like Elton John and the consecration of new sonic architects like Leon Thomas and MAG points to a future where innovation and legacy walk hand in hand, challenging the industry to be as diverse as the audience it serves.
